decisionMemorySummary.note: Aggregates Codex recommendation snapshots compared against final human workbench decisions. Missing historical Codex recommendations remain unavailable and are not inferred.

meta.note: Codex-first conservative review. Human QC still required before merge.

highRiskItems.reviewerReasoningSummary: Conflict between source extracted key (No) and top candidate q0636 (Right/Yes). Visual shows amber light and a stop line; interpretation (may continue if already past line) is jurisdiction-dependent and ambiguous here — cannot safely map to existing qid.
highRiskItems.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top matcher q0636 conflicts with source key/interpretation; visual/state ambiguous.

highRiskItems.reviewerReasoningSummary: Question asks legal penalty points for drunk driving but no authoritative local mapping present; machine candidates relate to other offences. Legal fact cannot be inferred from provided evidence.
highRiskItems.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top machine candidate (q0981) concerns forged/altered license, not drunk driving — not a reliable match.

highRiskItems.reviewerReasoningSummary: POV road image contains no visible speed-limit sign; no reliable way to map pictured scene to one of the numeric options. Candidate matches differ widely — cannot approve.

Source answer B = 'réduction les voies de circulation' (lane reduction) is valid. Candidate matches are mixed/low confidence so create new question to preserve correct localized phrasing.

rejectedTopMatcherExamples.reviewerReasoningSummary: Image shows police hand signals (two officers) but gesture semantics are ambiguous in the image and do not clearly map to any single existing qid or to one of the four localized options.
Blue rectangular sign with white U-turn arrow = U-turn lane. Master q0770 corresponds to U-turn lane; localized option B ('faire demi-tour') matches that meaning.
rejectedTopMatcherExamples.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top automated match (q0878) indicated straight-going lane which conflicts with the visible U-turn arrow; therefore q0878 was not accepted.

rejectedTopMatcherExamples.reviewerReasoningSummary: Conflicting candidate qids: one asserts the statement is Wrong (immediate return after overtaking) while another asserts Right (signal when returning). The French wording is ambiguous about timing; cannot reliably map to a single master qid.

Source statement = when pedestrians suddenly appear you must slow and yield. q0020 prompt explicitly says reduce speed and evade for abruptly crossing pedestrians. Good semantic match. Machine top q0017 is about high beams (not relevant).
rejectedTopMatcherExamples.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top machine match (q0017) concerns high-beam glare at night; q0020 better matches sudden pedestrian crossing.

rejectedTopMatcherExamples.reviewerReasoningSummary: Image shows dashboard defrost/defog symbols; source answer A = 'Le système de désembuage' matches. Top machine candidates concern lighting/fan symbols (q0598/q0597) and do not clearly represent a defogger control — likely a new localized dashboard-item is needed.
rejectedTopMatcherExamples.rejectedTopMatcherReason: Top machine matches (q0598/q0597) refer to light switches or fan modes, not the defogger symbol visible in the image.

rejectedTopMatcherExamples.reviewerReasoningSummary: Source: when exiting highway reduce speed in deceleration lane before entering ramp. q0041 specifically states reduce speed in deceleration lane before entering ramp; good match.

rejectedTopMatcherExamples.reviewerReasoningSummary: Source item lists three ways to warn before overtaking (left indicator, flash high/low beams, honk) — this exactly matches q0238; ROW 'Yes' corresponds to source option A.

No source image available and prompt is generic ('Que indique le marquage au sol ?'). Candidate matches are weak and divergent (prohibitory vs indicative vs directional). Cannot confirm correct local option without visual marking.

Statement about criminal liability for using a counterfeit licence may be true but candidate matches mix administrative penalty/points items. Nuance between criminal vs administrative sanction not resolved by candidates; do not approve automatically.

Statement about not braking immediately after a tire air leak is ambiguous; candidate matches show conflicting guidance about emergency braking vs gradual control. Without clearer match or image, keep unresolved.
Source correct option ('ne pas troubler le flux régulier des véhicules') semantically matches q0387 option 'cannot interfere other vehicles' (q0387 correctKey=C). That maps to French option B here.

Source correct key indicates 'driver not fastened seatbelt' (C) but top machine candidates point at door/window issues (B). No clear existing qid matching the seatbelt variant; keep unresolved to avoid mis-assignment.

items.reviewerReasoningSummary: Image shows red car changing lane; top candidate q0236 specifically asks whether the red car's lane change is correct and marks it Wrong. Visual semantics and prompt match; source options A=Yes/B=No, so locale key B (No).
Blue circular mandatory right-turn arrow visible; q0868 means 'turn right'. Source option D = 'tourner à droite' so map to D.

items.reviewerReasoningSummary: Text-only MCQ about rest after >4 hours; q0445 matches and has correct answer '20 minutes'. Source option D = '20 minutes' so map to D.
Prompt states 'ON position prevents use of electronic equipment' which is false; q0550 has correctRow Wrong -> localized option B (No). Image of ignition key supports match.
Red-bordered triangular sign with Chinese text appears to mean 'reduce speed and yield' / 'ralentir et céder le passage' (source option D). q0816 maps to that meaning — map to D.

Image shows police hand signals; q0896's correctOption indicates 'change lane'. Source option C = 'changer de voie' matches that meaning. Some candidate ambiguity across languages so moderate confidence.
Triangular warning sign with bicycle symbol = 'attention aux véhicules non motorisés' (source D). q0838 correctOptionText 'watch for non-vehicles' matches; map to D.
Text-only highway lane question; q0143 states vehicles >110 km/h should use far left lane. Source option A = 'La première voie à gauche' matches; approve and map to A.
Image shows police hand signals (two officers) but gesture semantics are ambiguous in the image and do not clearly map to any single existing qid or to one of the four localized options.
Blue rectangular sign with white U-turn arrow = U-turn lane. Master q0770 corresponds to U-turn lane; localized option B ('faire demi-tour') matches that meaning.

items.reviewerReasoningSummary: Dashboard icon: car outline with internal arrow indicates air internal circulation. Master q0623 is 'air internal circulation' and localized option B ('la circulation intérieure de l'air') matches.
Green highway service-area sign (P, fuel, fork/knife) = highway service area ahead. Master q0884 corresponds to service area; localized option B matches.
Asks length of probationary period after obtaining license; master q0508 correct = 12 months. Local option B is 12 mois.
Image/scene and options concern license-plate violations. Master q0476 correct = 'deliberately cover the license plate'. Localized option C explicitly matches that meaning.
Yellow-black striped marking at tunnel entrance indicates tunnel warning; master q0734 corresponds to tunnel sign. Local option D ('attention au tunnel') matches meaning.
Statement 'You must not make a U-turn on the highway access ramp' matches master ROW q0010 which is True/Right.

items.reviewerReasoningSummary: Statement claims continuously flashing yellow means you may speed up and pass. q0647 covers this statement and marks it Wrong (should not speed up). Direct semantic match.

Source: when exiting highway reduce speed in deceleration lane before entering ramp. q0041 specifically states reduce speed in deceleration lane before entering ramp; good match.

items.reviewerReasoningSummary: Blue information sign with 'P', bench and nature symbols — suggests a rest area. q0892 maps to 'rest area' (master key A). In localized options, 'aire de repos' is C, so map to C. Some ambiguity with observation deck/service area increases risk.
Image shows a U-turn arrow struck through (prohibition). q0669 correct meaning = 'no U-turn'. Localized option B = 'défense de faire demi-tour' matches.
Blue circular sign with straight and right arrows = allowed to go straight or turn right. q0746 master correct = 'go straight or turn right'. Local option B corresponds to that meaning.

Icon is a key in a rectangle — semantically matches a door lock/unlock control. Top candidate q0624 covers the same switch function; source option D corresponds to 'door unlock'. Close runner-up (child safety lock) creates some ambiguity.

items.reviewerReasoningSummary: Source statement about preserving scene and reporting when vehicle lacks plate/inspection/insurance matches q0222. For ROW options, 'Yes' is the correct/Right response (source A).
Question about correct behaviour in a traffic jam (wait in queue) matches q0118; source option D 'attendre dans la queue' maps to that correct answer.
Source item lists three ways to warn before overtaking (left indicator, flash high/low beams, honk) — this exactly matches q0238; ROW 'Yes' corresponds to source option A.

No source image available and prompt is generic ('Que indique le marquage au sol ?'). Candidate matches are weak and divergent (prohibitory vs indicative vs directional). Cannot confirm correct local option without visual marking.
Text exactly matches q0066 semantics: observe surroundings before starting. French options A=Yes/B=No so correct = A (Right/True).

Statement about criminal liability for using a counterfeit licence may be true but candidate matches mix administrative penalty/points items. Nuance between criminal vs administrative sanction not resolved by candidates; do not approve automatically.
Prompt and options match q0504 (unmanned railway crossing). Correct behaviour = stop and check before crossing, corresponds to French option C.

Statement about not braking immediately after a tire air leak is ambiguous; candidate matches show conflicting guidance about emergency braking vs gradual control. Without clearer match or image, keep unresolved.

Top candidate q0090 is the same proposition phrased in English. q0090 labels that emergency braking in a tyre-blowout scenario is Wrong — so French option B (No) corresponds to the approved master answer.

Source correct option ('ne pas troubler le flux régulier des véhicules') semantically matches q0387 option 'cannot interfere other vehicles' (q0387 correctKey=C). That maps to French option B here.
